pairs.question: When and where was Byron Shutt born?
pairs.answer: Byron Shutt was born on October 26, 1955, in Canada.

pairs.question: Which teams drafted Byron Shutt in 1975?
pairs.answer: Byron Shutt was drafted by the Pittsburgh Penguins in the ninth round of the 1975 NHL Amateur Draft and by the Toronto Toros in the seventh round of the 1975 WHA Amateur Draft.

pairs.question: What were the two teams Byron Shutt played for during his professional hockey career in the 1978–79 WHA season?
pairs.answer: Byron Shutt played for the Cincinnati Stingers during the 1978–79 WHA season.

pairs.question: Why did Byron Shutt choose to play in the WHA instead of the NHL after being drafted in 1975?
pairs.answer: Byron Shutt opted to play in the WHA after being selected by the Toronto Toros, despite also being drafted by the Pittsburgh Penguins in the NHL.

pairs.question: What teams did Byron Shutt play for after the WHA folded in 1979?
pairs.answer: After the WHA folded, his rights were claimed by the Hartford Whalers in the WHA dispersal draft. He played for both the Hartford Whalers and the Cincinnati Stingers' affiliate Springfield Indians during the 1979–80 season.

pairs.question: How many penalty minutes did Byron Shutt accumulate during his time with the Flint Generals?
pairs.answer: Byron Shutt racked up 235 penalty minutes while playing for the Flint Generals of the International Hockey League (IHL).

pairs.question: What did Byron Shutt do after retiring from professional hockey?
pairs.answer: After retiring, Byron Shutt operated a landscaping firm and coached hockey for Bay High School in Ohio.

pairs.question: Which youth hockey tournament did Byron Shutt participate in as a child?
pairs.answer: As a youth, Byron Shutt played in the 1968 Quebec International Pee-Wee Hockey Tournament with the Toronto Shopsy's minor ice hockey team.
